fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: California Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity california, how to transfer physical therapist license to california, physical therapist compact california, california physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Physical Therapy Board of California
board.parent_dept: Title Protection Only (Business and Professions Code Section 2585)

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in California.

steps.title: Create BreEZe Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register on the BreEZe online services portal (breeze.ca.gov), log in, select "Start a New Application" for Physical Therapist, and submit the application along with the $300 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited physical therapy program to the Physical Therapy Board of California.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions on the PTBC website to complete a Live Scan (for CA residents) or submit fingerprint cards (for out-of-state applicants) for a criminal background check.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Once authorized by the PTBC after application review, register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and pass the NPTE.

steps.title: Pass the California Law Examination (CLE)
steps.description: After receiving authorization from the PTBC, schedule and pass the state-specific California Law Examination.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a California physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $300. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers physical therapist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to California?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 8 to 9 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in California
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in California, submit your application and required documents through the state's BreEZe Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To apply for a Physical Therapist license, visit breeze.ca.gov. Select "New User? Register Here" and follow the prompts to create your account. Once registered, log in, select "Start a New Application," and search for the "Physical Therapist" application under the Physical Therapy Board of California.
